entry.title: Expiring HTLCs without free relay
entry.summary: The concept of grinding and rebroadcasting within programming projects is addressed with a proposal aimed at eliminating the need for these processes. However, it's suggested that maintaining these procedures could still be beneficial as a precautionary measure for enhancing system robustness and providing a deeper layer of defense against failures or errors in games. The intent behind the proposal was to streamline operations and improve efficiency, yet there's an acknowledgment of the potential value in retaining older methods as part of a comprehensive strategy to ensure system integrity and reliability. This approach underlines the importance of balancing innovation with proven practices to safeguard against unforeseen issues.
